skillsSnapshot.prompt:
The following skills provide specialized instructions for specific tasks.
Use the read tool to load a skill's file when the task matches its description.
When a skill file references a relative path, resolve it against the skill directory (parent of SKILL.md / dirname of the path) and use that absolute path in tool commands.

<available_skills>
  <skill>
    <name>browser-automation</name>
    <description>Use when controlling web pages with the OpenClaw browser tool, especially multi-step flows, login checks, tab management, or recovery from stale refs/timeouts.</description>
    <location>~/.openclaw/plugin-skills/browser-automation/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>canvas</name>
    <description>Present HTML on connected OpenClaw node canvases, navigate/eval/snapshot, and debug canvas host URLs.</description>
    <location>~/.nvm/versions/node/v24.16.0/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/canvas/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>diagram-maker</name>
    <description>Create SVG/HTML or Excalidraw diagrams for concepts, architecture, flows, and whiteboards.</description>
    <location>~/.nvm/versions/node/v24.16.0/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/diagram-maker/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>healthcheck</name>
    <description>Audit/harden OpenClaw hosts: SSH, firewall, updates, exposure, backups, disk encryption, gateway security.</description>
    <location>~/.nvm/versions/node/v24.16.0/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/healthcheck/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>meme-maker</name>
    <description>Search meme templates, suggest formats, and generate local or hosted image memes.</description>
    <location>~/.nvm/versions/node/v24.16.0/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/meme-maker/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>node-connect</name>
    <description>Diagnose OpenClaw Android, iOS, or macOS node pairing, QR/setup code, route, auth, and connection failures.</description>
    <location>~/.nvm/versions/node/v24.16.0/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/node-connect/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>node-inspect-debugger</name>
    <description>Debug Node.js with node inspect, --inspect, breakpoints, CDP, heap, and CPU profiles.</description>
    <location>~/.nvm/versions/node/v24.16.0/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/node-inspect-debugger/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>notion</name>
    <description>Notion CLI/API for pages, Markdown content, data sources, files, comments, search, Workers, and raw API calls.</description>
    <location>~/.nvm/versions/node/v24.16.0/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/notion/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>python-debugpy</name>
    <description>Debug Python with pdb, breakpoint(), post-mortem inspection, and debugpy remote attach.</description>
    <location>~/.nvm/versions/node/v24.16.0/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/python-debugpy/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>skill-creator</name>
    <description>Create, edit, audit, tidy, validate, or restructure AgentSkills and SKILL.md files.</description>
    <location>~/.nvm/versions/node/v24.16.0/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/skill-creator/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>spike</name>
    <description>Run throwaway prototypes to validate feasibility, compare approaches, and report a verdict.</description>
    <location>~/.nvm/versions/node/v24.16.0/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/spike/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>taskflow</name>
    <description>Coordinate multi-step detached tasks as one durable TaskFlow job with owner context, state, waits, and child tasks.</description>
    <location>~/.nvm/versions/node/v24.16.0/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/taskflow/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>taskflow-inbox-triage</name>
    <description>Example TaskFlow pattern for inbox triage, intent routing, waiting on replies, and later summaries.</description>
    <location>~/.nvm/versions/node/v24.16.0/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/taskflow-inbox-triage/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>tmux</name>
    <description>Control tmux sessions/panes for interactive CLIs: list, capture output, send keys, paste text, monitor prompts.</description>
    <location>~/.nvm/versions/node/v24.16.0/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/tmux/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>weather</name>
    <description>Current weather and forecasts with wttr.in via curl for locations, rain, temperature, travel planning.</description>
    <location>~/.nvm/versions/node/v24.16.0/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/weather/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
</available_skills>

compactionCheckpoints.summary:
## Goal
The user wants to create a 3D model of an object using Blender, leveraging the Blender MCP addon running on port 9876, with a provided reference photo.

## Constraints & Preferences
- The 3D modeling process must utilize the Blender MCP port 9876 interface.
- The primary reference material is the attached photo (keychain figure).
- The model creation requires analyzing the provided reference photo for geometry, dimensions, and components.

## Progress
### Done
- Initial attempts were made to use reference images (271.jpg, diagram images), but 271.jpg was not found locally.
- The system successfully analyzed a diagram using the `image` tool, identifying key geometric elements for an optical system model.
- The user was informed that the previous attached file was a video, not an image.

### In Progress
- Analyzing the new reference photo (keychain figure) to guide the 3D model creation process in Blender.

### Blocked
- The previous reference image "reference 271.jpg" could not be located in the workspace.
- The initial request sequence involved switching between diagram references and character photo references, requiring confirmation on which reference to use for the final model.

## Key Decisions
- **Reference Image**: The current, most relevant reference for the 3D model appears to be the keychain figure in the latest attached image (`media://inbound/2c1b7157-1960-47f6-9ebd-c7c1e809a7de.jpg`), rather than the previously mentioned "reference 271.jpg".
- **Modeling Approach**: The modeling should proceed by analyzing the components of the attached photo (Hardware, Main Character, Accessory) and mapping their geometry to Blender using the MCP addon capabilities.

## Next Steps
1. Analyze the attached keychain image (`media://inbound/2c1b7157-1960-47f6-9ebd-c7c1e809a7de.jpg`) in detail using the vision model to extract precise geometric specifications for 3D modeling.
2. Outline a step-by-step workflow instructing the user on how to set up Blender (including ensuring the MCP addon is running on port 9876) to model the identified components (Clasp, Character, Accessory) based on the analysis.

## Critical Context
- **Goal Toolset**: Blender, MCP addon (Port 9876).
- **Current Reference**: The keychain figure photo (`media://inbound/2c1b7157-1960-47f6-9ebd-c7c1e809a7de.jpg`).
- **Previous Reference Context**: The previous diagram analysis provided a detailed structure for an optical system model.

---

**Turn Context (split turn):**

## Original Request
The user wants to use the attached image (a keychain figure of a man holding a red lobster/crab) as a reference to create a 3D model in Blender.

## Early Progress
- The AI recognized the task was 3D modeling based on a reference image for a figurine.
- The AI called an `image` tool to analyze the attached JPEG, prompting for a structured breakdown for Blender modeling.

## Context for Suffix
- The tool execution returned a detailed, comprehensive analysis suggesting an *expressive stylization* approach, breaking the model into five key components (Human Character, Head/Hair/Face, Suit, etc.). This analysis provided technical modeling suggestions (Box Modeling, Sculpting) for each part.

<read-files>
/home/tpk/.openclaw/workspace/AGENTS.md
</read-files>

<modified-files>
/home/tpk/.openclaw/workspace/HEARTBEAT.md
</modified-files>

- The system successfully analyzed a diagram using the `image` tool, identifying key geometric elements for an optical system model.
- The user was informed that the previous attached file was a video, not an image.
- An analysis of the new reference photo (keychain figure) has been completed and provided modeling suggestions.

### In Progress
- Outline a step-by-step workflow instructing the user on how to set up Blender (including ensuring the MCP addon is running on port 9876) to model the identified components (Clasp, Character, Accessory) based on the analysis.

- **Modeling Approach**: The modeling should proceed by analyzing the components of the attached photo (Hardware, Main Character, Accessory) and mapping their geometry to Blender using the MCP addon capabilities.

## Next Steps
1. Outline a step-by-step workflow instructing the user on how to set up Blender (including ensuring the MCP addon is running on port 9876) to model the identified components (Clasp, Character, Accessory) based on the analysis.

## Critical Context
- **Goal Toolset**: Blender, MCP addon (Port 9876).
